title: HbA1c Measurement Protocol
author: Peter Havel
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.ynrfvd6
materials: Mix R1a and R1b reagents together in a 70:30 ratio.

steps.title: Use 250 µl of lysis buffer...
steps.instruction: Use 250 µl of lysis buffer to lyse 20 µl samples of whole blood and calibrators. IMPORTANT: Make sure the samples are totally lysed. Any solid material floating around will interfere with reading in the platereader.

steps.title: Add 25 µl of each calibrator...
steps.instruction: Add 25 µl of each calibrator and sample to each well.

steps.title: Add 160 µl of reagent R1ab mix to each well
steps.instruction: Add 160 µl of reagent R1ab mix to each well. Incubate at 37°C for 5 minutes then read at 720 nm. IMPORTANT: Make sure not to add any bubbles to the wells when dispensing reagents, this will interfere with reading in the platereader.

steps.title: Add 70 µl of R2 to each well
steps.instruction: Add 70 µl of R2 to each well. Incubate at 37°C for 3 minutes then read at 720 nm.

steps.title: Subtract blank readings from final readings
steps.instruction: Subtract blank readings from final readings. The assay will be linear so the unknown samples can be calculated as (Sample Absorbance ÷ Calibrator Absorbance) × Calibrator Concentration.
